stats.fact: Cursor 2.0 supports up to 8 simultaneous AI agents working on the same codebase via git worktrees
Composer model runs at ~250 tokens/second, completing most tasks in under 30 seconds — 4x faster than comparable intelligence models
Composer 2 scores 61.3 on CursorBench vs 44.2 for Composer 1.5 — a 39% improvement
Terminal-Bench 2.0: Composer 2 scores 61.7, surpassing Claude Opus 4.6 at 58.0 (GPT-5.4 leads at 75.1)
Cursor 3.2 released April 24, 2026 introduced /multitask — automatic task decomposition into async subagents running in parallel
95% of developers use AI tools at least weekly in 2026; 75% use AI for more than half of their coding work
Cursor agentic tasks are 30% faster with parallel background agents compared to sequential execution
Background Agents run in isolated AWS VMs with internet access, available on Cursor Pro ($20/month) and above
Cursor Supermaven autocomplete achieves 72% acceptance rate among developers
Compaction-in-the-loop RL in Composer 2 reduces compaction errors by 50% and uses one-fifth of the tokens vs prompt-based summarization
